title: Two typhoons approach Japan with heavy rain and winds
summary: Typhoon 7 approaches Okinawa and Kyushu while Typhoon 8 weakens, but torrential rains will affect wide areas of the country.

title: Japanese government sets investment of 370 trillion yen in 17 strategic areas
summary: The growth plan includes 10 trillion yen annually in additional fiscal spending, but lacks an independent fiscal oversight body.

title: Fire at Tokyo school caused by electric heater near wet clothing
summary: A teacher stated she was drying clothes in the preparation room, which started the fire that damaged the building without causing casualties.

title: Convicted in murder of student in Asahikawa accepts 27-year sentence and will not appeal
summary: The defense of the 23-year-old accused announced it will not appeal the ruling, closing the judicial process for the homicide of a young woman in Hokkaido.

title: Special investigation prosecutor to be tried for illegal interrogation
summary: The Tokyo District Court determined that the prosecutor violated the law by shouting at the suspect not to blame others for his silence.

title: Cool Japan accumulates losses of 54 billion yen and government studies its merger
summary: The Ministry of Economy evaluates the integration of the cultural promotion agency after its constant deficits, despite state investment of 140 billion.

title: LDP and Innovation present joint proposal to revise the three security documents
summary: Both parties submitted their recommendations to the Prime Minister to update the defense strategy, although differences persist between them.

title: Proposal to reduce consumption tax includes 1% rate for food from 2027
summary: The intermediate plan does not detail the financing sources and proposes support for agriculture and restaurants, without consensus on its viability.

title: China detains two Japanese for suspected smuggling of rare earth products
summary: The detainees, linked to Fuji Electric, allegedly tried to take processed materials out of the country illegally, the Chinese government confirmed.

title: Japanese national football team fine-tunes its strategy for the World Cup with support from Chinese fans
summary: The Japanese team prepares for the round of 16 with tactical flexibility, while Chinese supporters show respect towards Japan during the tournament.

title: Emperor and Empress of Japan make state visit to Belgium and the Netherlands
summary: The imperial couple participated in official ceremonies and highlighted four generations of ties between the royal families.

title: 92 dishes candidates to be the 'victory meal' of shogi champion Fujii Sota
summary: The city of Hamamatsu presented the culinary proposals for the king title tournament, in a competition seeking the ideal menu.

title: Masayoshi Son wants to remain at the helm of SoftBank and proposes alliance with TEPCO
summary: At the shareholders' meeting, the founder of SoftBank expressed his desire to continue for another 10 or 15 years and spoke of energy collaboration for AI centers.

title: Temporary maritime corridor established in the Strait of Hormuz to evacuate thousands of people
summary: In the face of declining maritime traffic and geopolitical risk, IMO and UN coordinate the departure of more than 10,000 people from the region.

title: KADOKAWA reelects its president despite opposition from main shareholder Oasis
summary: The shareholders' meeting rejected the activists' proposals, while other companies like Rohto and Yakult also faced similar initiatives.

title: Ex-AKB48 member Mei Hanada shaves head and promises to keep fighting
summary: The 22-year-old young woman explained through tears that she will continue her entertainment career after the termination of her contract.

title: Record heat wave in Europe: France reaches 44.3°C and dozens of deaths recorded
summary: Italy banned agricultural and construction work during the day, while the heat wave breaks historical records in several European countries.

title: Local initiatives in Japan seek to halt mass closure of bookstores
summary: Several municipalities offer subsidies and create 'public bookstores' to maintain access to books in areas without establishments.

title: JERA to resume coal-fired electricity sales and launches new menu for retailers
summary: The energy company responds to the crisis in the Middle East and the increase in LNG prices with new business strategies to stabilize prices.

title: Nissan announces voluntary buyouts and faces rejection of reelection of a director
summary: The Japanese automaker offers early retirement to administrative employees while a major shareholder votes against an outside director.
